texto: I have worked like a dog all my life, honey. Dancing, as Fred Astaire said, is next to ditch-digging. You sweat and you slave and the audience doesn’t think you have a brain in your head.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ann Miller: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “I have worked like a dog all my life, honey. Dancing, as Fred Astaire said, is next to ditch-digg…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, dance, worked pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e trabalho.
